candidates.item: JMI is our technology partner in the Design, Architecture and Development of an intelligent energy management platform and a smart lighting energy management solution. These are hosted by JMI and offered as a SaaS solution to over twenty five active utility customers around the World.
candidates.why_interesting: A pure IT services firm that also *owns and hosts* a live SaaS product — an energy management + smart lighting platform — is unusual; most IT services shops only build for clients, never operate their own product at scale. Twenty-five active global utility customers is a real, verifiable number behind the claim.
candidates.recency_note: Pulled from JMI's homepage testimonial section; date of testimonial not specified.

candidates.item: Save energy and reduce costs by up to 40% with Property Smart Grid.
candidates.why_interesting: JMI's own energy platform (Property Smart Grid) carries a specific, bold performance claim — 40% energy cost reduction — rather than a vague promise. A concrete outcome figure from a vendor's own product page is notable and conversation-worthy.
candidates.recency_note: From JMI's Products page; no date stamp.

candidates.item: The scalability of this solution will enable deployment to more than 1000 retail locations in the year to come.
candidates.why_interesting: A client testimonial on JMI's homepage cites a 1,000+ retail location rollout as the scope of a single JMI engagement. That's a striking scale signal for what looks externally like a mid-sized firm (~158–500 employees), making it a surprising mismatch worth noting.
candidates.recency_note: Verbatim client quote on JMI homepage; original project date not specified.

candidates.item: Currently, there are over 350 million daily users of products and services delivered by JMI.
candidates.why_interesting: 350 million DAU is an enormous end-user footprint for a firm whose public employee count sits in the low hundreds. If accurate, it implies JMI's platforms and embedded software touch a huge slice of consumer/enterprise life invisibly — a striking contrast to their low brand profile.
candidates.recency_note: Sourced from a JMI press release dated July 16, 2024 (Michael Ching appointment), as quoted on CB Insights.

candidates.item: Prior to starting JMI, Shawn was instrumental in implementing various Banking and Investment Banking products and solutions for organizations like Citigroup and Goldman Sachs.
candidates.why_interesting: The CEO/co-founder came from Goldman Sachs and Citigroup as an enterprise architect — a pedigree that explains JMI's heavy financial-services client base and credibility in that sector. It's a distinctive founding backstory (Wall Street architect turns IT firm founder) versus the typical consulting-firm origin story.
candidates.recency_note: From JMI's own Leadership page; biographical detail, effectively evergreen.

candidates.item: GT Logistics bridges the gap between ground transportation companies, corporate travel departments, and retail passengers.
candidates.why_interesting: In addition to the energy SaaS product, JMI has built a second, entirely different proprietary SaaS product — a ground-transportation logistics platform. Two distinct owned-and-operated SaaS products from a firm typically described as an IT services provider is genuinely unexpected.
candidates.recency_note: From JMI's Products page; no date stamp.

candidates.item: Marimanickam M — Software Developer at Jean Martin Inc., joined in 2019. Prior experience: Internship Trainee at Vamsoft Technology (December 2017 – May 2018). Education: Thiagarajar College of Engineering, attended 2015–2018.
candidates.why_interesting: Thiagarajar College of Engineering (Madurai, Tamil Nadu) is a well-regarded institution in South India — the same region as JMI's Chennai office. The career path (TCE → short Vamsoft internship → JMI) is a concrete, humanizing detail about the individual recipient.
candidates.recency_note: The Org profile; join date listed as 2019 but profile freshness is unknown.
